extra.message: User input from request.form['expression'] is passed directly to eval() without any sanitization. This allows arbitrary Python code execution on the server.

cwe: CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

extra.message: User input from request.form['address'] is concatenated into a shell command string passed to popen(). The rp() helper accepts unsanitized user input that is appended to an nslookup command, enabling OS command injection.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command ('OS Command Injection')

extra.message: User input from request.form['name'] is embedded into a template string using Python % formatting (line 189), then the entire template including user input is rendered via render_template_string() (line 205). This allows server-side template injection (SSTI) where an attacker can inject Jinja2 expressions like {{config}}, {{self.__class__.__mro__}} to access internal server objects and achieve remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-1336: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements Used in a Template Engine

extra.message: XML parser is configured with no_network=False and dtd_validation=True (line 157), then user-supplied XML from request.form['xml'] is parsed via etree.fromstring() (line 159). This configuration does not disable external entity processing, allowing XML External Entity (XXE) attacks to read local files, perform SSRF, or cause denial of service.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: The result of eval(expression) — where expression is user-controlled — is embedded directly into raw HTML using string concatenation without any HTML escaping. Even without exploiting the eval() for code execution, an attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: User input from request.form['name'] is embedded into raw HTML via % formatting on line 189 without any escaping. The resulting string is placed directly into the HTML body outside any Jinja2 expression tags, so it bypasses auto-escaping when rendered via render_template_string().

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: The Flask secret key is hardcoded as a literal string 'Harry Potter And The Deathly Hallows' directly in the source code. This key is used to sign session cookies; anyone with access to the source code can forge valid sessions.

Flask debug mode is enabled (app.debug = True) in the main block. This exposes the Werkzeug interactive debugger, which allows arbitrary code execution on the server and leaks sensitive information through detailed tracebacks.

cwe: CWE-215: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Debugging Code

extra.message: The User model stores passwords as plain strings (db.Column(db.String(80))) in the database without any hashing. Passwords are set directly from user input (lines 49, 62) and compared in plaintext (line 50), violating secure password storage practices.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: The Flask development server binds to host='0.0.0.0' (all network interfaces), exposing the application to all network connections. Combined with debug mode, this makes the Werkzeug debugger accessible from any network.

cwe: CWE-1327: Binding to an Unrestricted IP Address
